NKJV.1: Then He went out from there and came to His own country, and His disciples followed Him.
NKJV.2: And when the Sabbath had come, He began to teach in the synagogue. And many hearing Him were astonished, saying, “Where did this Man get these things? And what wisdom is this which is given to Him, that such mighty works are performed by His hands!

NKJV.4: But Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor except in his own country, among his own relatives, and in his own house.”

NKJV.7: And He called the twelve to Himself, and began to send them out two by two, and gave them power over unclean spirits.

NKJV.9: but to wear sandals, and not to put on two tunics.
NKJV.10: Also He said to them, “In whatever place you enter a house, stay there till you depart from that place.

NKJV.12: So they went out and preached that people should repent.
NKJV.13: And they cast out many demons, and anointed with oil many who were sick, and healed them.

NKJV.20: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a just and holy man, and he protected him. And when he heard him, he did many things, and heard him gladly.

ESV.1: He went away from there and came to his hometown, and his disciples followed him.
ESV.2: And on the Sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things? What is the wisdom given to him? How are such mighty works done by his hands?
ESV.3: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary and brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? And are not his sisters here with us?” And they took offense at him.
ESV.4: And Jesus said to them,  “A prophet is not without honor, except in his hometown and among his relatives and in his own household.”
ESV.5: And he could do no mighty work there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
ESV.6: And he marveled because of their unbelief. And he went about among the villages teaching.
ESV.7: And he called the twelve and began to send them out two by two, and gave them authority over the unclean spirits.
ESV.8: He charged them to take nothing for their journey except a staff — no bread, no bag, no money in their belts —
ESV.9: but to wear sandals and not put on two tunics.
ESV.10: And he said to them, “Whenever you enter a house, stay there until you depart from there.
ESV.11: And if any place will not receive you and they will not listen to you, when you leave, shake off the dust that is on your feet as a testimony against them.”
ESV.12: So they went out and proclaimed that people should repent.
ESV.13: And they cast out many demons and anointed with oil many who were sick and healed them.
ESV.14: King Herod heard of it, for Jesus'  name had become known. Some  said, “John the Baptist  has been raised from the dead. That is why these miraculous powers are at work in him.”
ESV.15: But others said, “He is Elijah.” And others said, “He is a prophet, like one of the prophets of old.”
ESV.16: But when Herod heard of it, he said, “John, whom I beheaded, has been raised.”
ESV.17: For it was Herod who had sent and seized John and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife, because he had married her.
ESV.18: For John had been saying to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife.”
ESV.19: And Herodias had a grudge against him and wanted to put him to death. But she could not,
ESV.20: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he kept him safe. When he heard him, he was greatly perplexed, and yet he heard him gladly.
ESV.21: But an opportunity came when Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his nobles and military commanders and the leading men of Galilee.
ESV.22: For when Herodias's daughter came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests. And the king said to the girl, “Ask me for whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.”
ESV.23: And he vowed to her, “Whatever you ask me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom.”
ESV.24: And she went out and said to her mother, “For what should I ask?” And she said, “The head of John the Baptist.”
ESV.25: And she came in immediately with haste to the king and asked, saying, “I want you to give me at once the head of John the Baptist on a platter.”
ESV.26: And the king was exceedingly sorry, but because of his oaths and his guests he did not want to break his word to her.
ESV.27: And immediately the king sent an executioner with orders to bring John's  head. He went and beheaded him in the prison
ESV.28: and brought his head on a platter and gave it to the girl, and the girl gave it to her mother.
ESV.29: When his disciples heard of it, they came and took his body and laid it in a tomb.
ESV.30: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him all that they had done and taught.
ESV.31: And he said to them, “Come away by yourselves to a desolate place and rest a while.” For many were coming and going, and they had no leisure even to eat.
ESV.32: And they went away in the boat to a desolate place by themselves.
ESV.33: Now many saw them going and recognized them, and they ran there on foot from all the towns and got there ahead of them.
ESV.34: When he went ashore he saw a great crowd, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. And he began to teach them many things.
ESV.35: And when it grew late, his disciples came to him and said, “This is a desolate place, and the hour is now late.
ESV.36: Send them away to go into the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat.”
ESV.37: But he answered them,  “You give them something to eat.” And they said to him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii  worth of bread and give it to them to eat?”
ESV.38: And he said to them, “How many loaves do you have? Go and see.” And when they had found out, they said, “Five, and two fish.”
ESV.39: Then he commanded them all to sit down in groups on the green grass.
ESV.40: So they sat down in groups, by hundreds and by fifties.
ESV.41: And taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven and said a blessing and broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples to set before the people. And he divided the two fish among them all.
ESV.42: And they all ate and were satisfied.
ESV.43: And they took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and of the fish.
ESV.44: And those who ate the loaves were five thousand men.
ESV.45: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go before him to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
ESV.46: And after he had taken leave of them, he went up on the mountain to pray.
ESV.47: And when evening came, the boat was out on the sea, and he was alone on the land.
ESV.48: And he saw that they were making headway painfully, for the wind was against them. And about the fourth watch of the night  he came to them, walking on the sea. He meant to pass by them,
ESV.49: but when they saw him walking on the sea they thought it was a ghost, and cried out,
ESV.50: for they all saw him and were terrified. But immediately he spoke to them and said,  “Take heart; it is I. Do not be afraid.”
ESV.51: And he got into the boat with them, and the wind ceased. And they were utterly astounded,
ESV.52: for they did not understand about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.
ESV.53: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret and moored to the shore.
ESV.54: And when they got out of the boat, the people immediately recognized him
ESV.55: and ran about the whole region and began to bring the sick people on their beds to wherever they heard he was.
ESV.56: And wherever he came, in villages, cities, or countryside, they laid the sick in the marketplaces and implored him that they might touch even the fringe of his garment. And as many as touched it were made well.

NASB.1: Jesus went out from there and came into His hometown; and His disciples followed Him.
NASB.2: When the Sabbath came, He began to teach in the synagogue; and the many listeners were astonished, saying, 'Where did this man [get] these things, and what is [this] wisdom given to Him, and such miracles as these performed by His hands?
NASB.3: 'Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? Are not His sisters here with us?' And they took offense at Him.
NASB.4: Jesus said to them, 'A prophet is not without honor except in his hometown and among his [own] relatives and in his [own] household.'
NASB.5: And He could do no miracle there except that He laid His hands on a few sick people and healed them.
NASB.6: And He wondered at their unbelief. And He was going around the villages teaching.
NASB.7: And He summoned the twelve and began to send them out in pairs, and gave them authority over the unclean spirits;
NASB.8: and He instructed them that they should take nothing for [their] journey, except a mere staff-- no bread, no bag, no money in their belt--
NASB.9: but [to] wear sandals; and [He added], 'Do not put on two tunics.'
NASB.10: And He said to them, 'Wherever you enter a house, stay there until you leave town.
NASB.11: 'Any place that does not receive you or listen to you, as you go out from there, shake the dust off the soles of your feet for a testimony against them.'
NASB.12: They went out and preached that [men] should repent.
NASB.13: And they were casting out many demons and were anointing with oil many sick people and healing them.
NASB.14: And King Herod heard [of it], for His name had become well known; and [people] were saying, 'John the Baptist has risen from the dead, and that is why these miraculous powers are at work in Him.'
NASB.15: But others were saying, 'He is Elijah.' And others were saying, '[He is] a prophet, like one of the prophets [of old].'
NASB.16: But when Herod heard [of it], he kept saying, 'John, whom I beheaded, has risen!'
NASB.17: For Herod himself had sent and had John arrested and bound in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip, because he had married her.
NASB.18: For John had been saying to Herod, 'It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife.'
NASB.19: Herodias had a grudge against him and wanted to put him to death and could not [do so];
NASB.20: for Herod was afraid of John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he kept him safe. And when he heard him, he was very perplexed; but he used to enjoy listening to him.
NASB.21: A strategic day came when Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his lords and military commanders and the leading men of Galilee;
NASB.22: and when the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his dinner guests; and the king said to the girl, 'Ask me for whatever you want and I will give it to you.'
NASB.23: And he swore to her, 'Whatever you ask of me, I will give it to you; up to half of my kingdom.'
NASB.24: And she went out and said to her mother, 'What shall I ask for?' And she said, 'The head of John the Baptist.'
NASB.25: Immediately she came in a hurry to the king and asked, saying, 'I want you to give me at once the head of John the Baptist on a platter.'
NASB.26: And although the king was very sorry, [yet] because of his oaths and because of his dinner guests, he was unwilling to refuse her.
NASB.27: Immediately the king sent an executioner and commanded [him] to bring [back] his head. And he went and had him beheaded in the prison,
NASB.28: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl; and the girl gave it to her mother.
NASB.29: When his disciples heard [about this], they came and took away his body and laid it in a tomb.
NASB.30: The apostles gathered together with Jesus; and they reported to Him all that they had done and taught.
NASB.31: And He said to them, 'Come away by yourselves to a secluded place and rest a while.' (For there were many [people] coming and going, and they did not even have time to eat.)
NASB.32: They went away in the boat to a secluded place by themselves.
NASB.33: [The people] saw them going, and many recognized [them] and ran there together on foot from all the cities, and got there ahead of them.
NASB.34: When Jesus went ashore, He saw a large crowd, and He felt compassion for them because they were like sheep without a shepherd; and He began to teach them many things.
NASB.35: When it was already quite late, His disciples came to Him and said, 'This place is desolate and it is already quite late;
NASB.36: send them away so that they may go into the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat.'
NASB.37: But He answered them, 'You give them [something] to eat!' And they said to Him, 'Shall we go and spend two hundred denarii on bread and give them [something] to eat?'
NASB.38: And He said to them, 'How many loaves do you have? Go look!' And when they found out, they said, 'Five, and two fish.'
NASB.39: And He commanded them all to sit down by groups on the green grass.
NASB.40: They sat down in groups of hundreds and of fifties.
NASB.41: And He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up toward heaven, He blessed [the food] and broke the loaves and He kept giving [them] to the disciples to set before them; and He divided up the two fish among them all.
NASB.42: They all ate and were satisfied,
NASB.43: and they picked up twelve full baskets of the broken pieces, and also of the fish.
NASB.44: There were five thousand men who ate the loaves.
NASB.45: Immediately Jesus made His disciples get into the boat and go ahead of [Him] to the other side to Bethsaida, while He Himself was sending the crowd away.
NASB.46: After bidding them farewell, He left for the mountain to pray.
NASB.47: When it was evening, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and He was alone on the land.
NASB.48: Seeing them straining at the oars, for the wind was against them, at about the fourth watch of the night He came to them, walking on the sea; and He intended to pass by them.
NASB.49: But when they saw Him walking on the sea, they supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;
NASB.50: for they all saw Him and were terrified. But immediately He spoke with them and said to them, 'Take courage; it is I, do not be afraid.'
NASB.51: Then He got into the boat with them, and the wind stopped; and they were utterly astonished,
NASB.52: for they had not gained any insight from the [incident of] the loaves, but their heart was hardened.
NASB.53: When they had crossed over they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.
NASB.54: When they got out of the boat, immediately [the people] recognized Him,
NASB.55: and ran about that whole country and began to carry here and there on their pallets those who were sick, to the place they heard He was.
NASB.56: Wherever He entered villages, or cities, or countryside, they were laying the sick in the market places, and imploring Him that they might just touch the fringe of His cloak; and as many as touched it were being cured.

NLT.1: Jesus left that part of the country and returned with his disciples to Nazareth, his hometown.
NLT.2: The next Sabbath he began teaching in the synagogue, and many who heard him were amazed. They asked, “Where did he get all this wisdom and the power to perform such miracles?”
NLT.3: Then they scoffed, “He’s just a carpenter, the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon. And his sisters live right here among us.” They were deeply offended and refused to believe in him.
NLT.4: Then Jesus told them, “A prophet is honored everywhere except in his own hometown and among his relatives and his own family.”
NLT.5: And because of their unbelief, he couldn’t do any miracles among them except to place his hands on a few sick people and heal them.
NLT.6: And he was amazed at their unbelief. Jesus Sends Out the Twelve Disciples Then Jesus went from village to village, teaching the people.
NLT.7: And he called his twelve disciples together and began sending them out two by two, giving them authority to cast out evil spirits.
NLT.8: He told them to take nothing for their journey except a walking stick — no food, no traveler’s bag, no money.
NLT.9: He allowed them to wear sandals but not to take a change of clothes.
NLT.10: “Wherever you go,” he said, “stay in the same house until you leave town.
NLT.11: But if any place refuses to welcome you or listen to you, shake its dust from your feet as you leave to show that you have abandoned those people to their fate.”
NLT.12: So the disciples went out, telling everyone they met to repent of their sins and turn to God.
NLT.13: And they cast out many demons and healed many sick people, anointing them with olive oil.
NLT.14: Herod Antipas, the king, soon heard about Jesus, because everyone was talking about him. Some were saying, “This must be John the Baptist raised from the dead. That is why he can do such miracles.”
NLT.15: Others said, “He’s the prophet Elijah.” Still others said, “He’s a prophet like the other great prophets of the past.”
NLT.16: When Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “John, the man I beheaded, has come back from the dead.”
NLT.17: For Herod had sent soldiers to arrest and imprison John as a favor to Herodias. She had been his brother Philip’s wife, but Herod had married her.
NLT.18: John had been telling Herod, “It is against God’s law for you to marry your brother’s wife.”
NLT.19: So Herodias bore a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But without Herod’s approval she was powerless,
NLT.20: for Herod respected John; and knowing that he was a good and holy man, he protected him. Herod was greatly disturbed whenever he talked with John, but even so, he liked to listen to him.
NLT.21: Herodias’s chance finally came on Herod’s birthday. He gave a party for his high government officials, army officers, and the leading citizens of Galilee.
NLT.22: Then his daughter, also named Herodias, came in and performed a dance that greatly pleased Herod and his guests. “Ask me for anything you like,” the king said to the girl, “and I will give it to you.”
NLT.23: He even vowed, “I will give you whatever you ask, up to half my kingdom!”
NLT.24: She went out and asked her mother, “What should I ask for?” Her mother told her, “Ask for the head of John the Baptist!”
NLT.25: So the girl hurried back to the king and told him, “I want the head of John the Baptist, right now, on a tray!”
NLT.26: Then the king deeply regretted what he had said; but because of the vows he had made in front of his guests, he couldn’t refuse her.
NLT.27: So he immediately sent an executioner to the prison to cut off John’s head and bring it to him. The soldier beheaded John in the prison,
NLT.28: brought his head on a tray, and gave it to the girl, who took it to her mother.
NLT.29: When John’s disciples heard what had happened, they came to get his body and buried it in a tomb.
NLT.30: The apostles returned to Jesus from their ministry tour and told him all they had done and taught.
NLT.31: Then Jesus said, “Let’s go off by ourselves to a quiet place and rest awhile.” He said this because there were so many people coming and going that Jesus and his apostles didn’t even have time to eat.
NLT.32: So they left by boat for a quiet place, where they could be alone.
NLT.33: But many people recognized them and saw them leaving, and people from many towns ran ahead along the shore and got there ahead of them.
NLT.34: Jesus saw the huge crowd as he stepped from the boat, and he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. So he began teaching them many things.
NLT.35: Late in the afternoon his disciples came to him and said, “This is a remote place, and it’s already getting late.
NLT.36: Send the crowds away so they can go to the nearby farms and villages and buy something to eat.”
NLT.37: But Jesus said, “You feed them.” “With what?” they asked. “We’d have to work for months to earn enough money to buy food for all these people!”
NLT.38: “How much bread do you have?” he asked. “Go and find out.” They came back and reported, “We have five loaves of bread and two fish.”
NLT.39: Then Jesus told the disciples to have the people sit down in groups on the green grass.
NLT.40: So they sat down in groups of fifty or a hundred.
NLT.41: Jesus took the five loaves and two fish, looked up toward heaven, and blessed them. Then, breaking the loaves into pieces, he kept giving the bread to the disciples so they could distribute it to the people. He also divided the fish for everyone to share.
NLT.42: They all ate as much as they wanted,
NLT.43: and afterward, the disciples picked up twelve baskets of leftover bread and fish.
NLT.44: A total of 5,000 men and their families were fed.
NLT.45: Immediately after this, Jesus insisted that his disciples get back into the boat and head across the lake to Bethsaida, while he sent the people home.
NLT.46: After telling everyone good-bye, he went up into the hills by himself to pray.
NLT.47: Late that night, the disciples were in their boat in the middle of the lake, and Jesus was alone on land.
NLT.48: He saw that they were in serious trouble, rowing hard and struggling against the wind and waves. About three o’clock in the morning Jesus came toward them, walking on the water. He intended to go past them,
NLT.49: but when they saw him walking on the water, they cried out in terror, thinking he was a ghost.
NLT.50: They were all terrified when they saw him. But Jesus spoke to them at once. “Don’t be afraid,” he said. “Take courage! I am here! ”
NLT.51: Then he climbed into the boat, and the wind stopped. They were totally amazed,
NLT.52: for they still didn’t understand the significance of the miracle of the loaves. Their hearts were too hard to take it in.
NLT.53: After they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret. They brought the boat to shore
NLT.54: and climbed out. The people recognized Jesus at once,
NLT.55: and they ran throughout the whole area, carrying sick people on mats to wherever they heard he was.
NLT.56: Wherever he went — in villages, cities, or the countryside — they brought the sick out to the marketplaces. They begged him to let the sick touch at least the fringe of his robe, and all who touched him were healed.

WEB.1: He went out from there. He came into his own country, and his disciples followed him.
WEB.2: When the Sabbath had come, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things?” and, “What is the wisdom that is given to this man, that such mighty works come about by his hands?
WEB.3: Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James, Joses, Judah, and Simon? Aren’t his sisters here with us?” They were offended at him.
WEB.4: Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country, and among his own relatives, and in his own house.”
WEB.5: He could do no mighty work there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people, and healed them.
WEB.6: He marveled because of their unbelief. He went around the villages teaching.
WEB.7: He called to himself the twelve, and began to send them out two by two; and he gave them authority over the unclean spirits.
WEB.8: He commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, except a staff only: no bread, no wallet, no money in their purse,
WEB.9: but to wear sandals, and not put on two tunics.
WEB.10: He said to them, “Wherever you enter into a house, stay there until you depart from there.
WEB.11: Whoever will not receive you nor hear you, as you depart from there, shake off the dust that is under your feet for a testimony against them. Assuredly, I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!”
WEB.12: They went out and preached that people should repent.
WEB.13: They cast out many demons, and anointed many with oil who were sick, and healed them.
WEB.14: King Herod heard this, for his name had become known, and he said, “John the Baptizer has risen from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him.”
WEB.15: But others said, “He is Elijah.” Others said, “He is a prophet, or like one of the prophets.”
WEB.16: But Herod, when he heard this, said, “This is John, whom I beheaded. He has risen from the dead.”
WEB.17: For Herod himself had sent out and arrested John, and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, for he had married her.
WEB.18: For John said to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”
WEB.19: Herodias set herself against him, and desired to kill him, but she couldn’t,
WEB.20: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and kept him safe. When he heard him, he did many things, and he heard him gladly.
WEB.21: Then a convenient day came, that Herod on his birthday made a supper for his nobles, the high officers, and the chief men of Galilee.
WEB.22: When the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those sitting with him. The king said to the young lady, “Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you.”
WEB.23: He swore to her, “Whatever you shall ask of me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom.”
WEB.24: She went out, and said to her mother, “What shall I ask?” She said, “The head of John the Baptizer.”
WEB.25: She came in immediately with haste to the king, and asked, “I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptizer on a platter.”
WEB.26: The king was exceedingly sorry, but for the sake of his oaths, and of his dinner guests, he didn’t wish to refuse her.
WEB.27: Immediately the king sent out a soldier of his guard, and commanded to bring John’s head, and he went and beheaded him in the prison,
WEB.28: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the young lady; and the young lady gave it to her mother.
WEB.29: When his disciples heard this, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
WEB.30: The apostles gathered themselves together to Jesus, and they told him all things, whatever they had done, and whatever they had taught.
WEB.31: He said to them, “You come apart into a deserted place, and rest awhile.” For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
WEB.32: They went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.
WEB.33: They  saw them going, and many recognized him and ran there on foot from all the cities. They arrived before them and came together to him.
WEB.34: Jesus came out, saw a great multitude, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.
WEB.35: When it was late in the day, his disciples came to him, and said, “This place is deserted, and it is late in the day.
WEB.36: Send them away, that they may go into the surrounding country and villages, and buy themselves bread, for they have nothing to eat.”
WEB.37: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They asked him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii  worth of bread, and give them something to eat?”
WEB.38: He said to them, “How many loaves do you have? Go see.” When they knew, they said, “Five, and two fish.”
WEB.39: He commanded them that everyone should sit down in groups on the green grass.
WEB.40: They sat down in ranks, by hundreds and by fifties.
WEB.41: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves, and he gave to his disciples to set before them, and he divided the two fish among them all.
WEB.42: They all ate, and were filled.
WEB.43: They took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and also of the fish.
WEB.44: Those who ate the loaves were  five thousand men.
WEB.45: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he himself sent the multitude away.
WEB.46: After he had taken leave of them, he went up the mountain to pray.
WEB.47: When evening had come, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land.
WEB.48: Seeing them distressed in rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea,  and he would have passed by them,
WEB.49: but they, when they saw him walking on the sea, supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;
WEB.50: for they all saw him, and were troubled. But he immediately spoke with them, and said to them, “Cheer up! It is I!  Don’t be afraid.”
WEB.51: He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;
WEB.52: for they hadn’t understood about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.
WEB.53: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.
WEB.54: When they had come out of the boat, immediately the people recognized him,
WEB.55: and ran around that whole region, and began to bring those who were sick, on their mats, to where they heard he was.
WEB.56: Wherever he entered, into villages, or into cities, or into the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might touch just the fringe  of his garment; and as many as touched him were made well.

AMP.1: Jesus left there and came to His hometown [Nazareth]; and His disciples followed Him.
AMP.2: When the Sabbath came, He began to teach in the synagogue; and many who listened to Him were astonished, saying, "Where did this man get these things [this knowledge and spiritual insight]? What is this wisdom [this confident understanding of the Scripture] that has been given to Him, and such miracles as these performed by His hands?
AMP.3: Is this not the carpenter, the son of Mary, and the brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? Are His sisters not here with us?" And they were [deeply] offended by Him [and their disapproval blinded them to the fact that He was anointed by God as the Messiah].
AMP.4: Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not without honor (respect) except in his hometown and among his relatives and in his own household."
AMP.5: And He could not do a miracle there at all [because of their unbelief] except that He laid His hands on a few sick people and healed them.
AMP.6:
He wondered at their unbelief.
And He was going around in the villages teaching.
AMP.7: And He called the twelve [disciples] and began to send them out [as His special messengers] two by two, and gave them authority and power over the unclean spirits.
AMP.8: He told them to take nothing for the journey except a mere walking stick—no bread, no [traveler's] bag, no money in their belts—
AMP.9: but to wear sandals; and [He told them] not to wear two tunics.
AMP.10: And He told them, "Wherever you go into a house, stay there until you leave that town.
AMP.11: Any place that does not welcome you or listen to you, when you leave there, shake the dust off the soles of your feet as a testimony against them [breaking all ties with them because they rejected My message]."
AMP.12: So they went out and preached that men should repent [that is, think differently, recognize sin, turn away from it, and live changed lives].
AMP.13: And they were casting out many demons and were anointing with oil many who were sick, and healing them.
AMP.14: King Herod [Antipas] heard about this, for Jesus' name and reputation had become well known. People were saying, "John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and that is why these miraculous powers are at work in Him."
AMP.15: But others were saying, "He is Elijah!" And others were saying, "It is a prophet, like one of the prophets [of old]."
AMP.16: But when Herod heard [of it], he kept saying, "John, whom I beheaded, has risen [from the dead]!"
AMP.17: For Herod himself had sent [guards] and had John arrested and shackled in prison because of Herodias, the wife of his [half-] brother Philip, because he (Herod) had married her.
AMP.18: For John had been saying to Herod, "It is not lawful [under Mosaic Law] for you to have your brother's wife."
AMP.19: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him, but she could not,
AMP.20: because Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he continually kept him safe. When he heard John [speak], he was very perplexed; but he enjoyed listening to him.
AMP.21: But an opportune time [finally] came [for Herodias]. Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his officials (nobles, courtiers) and military commanders and the leading men of Galilee.
AMP.22: Now [Salome] the daughter of Herodias came in and danced [for the men]. She pleased and beguiled Herod and his dinner guests; and the king said to the girl, "Ask me for whatever you want and I will give it to you."
AMP.23: And he swore to her, "Whatever you ask me, I will give it to you; up to half of my kingdom."
AMP.24: She went out and said to her mother, "What shall I ask for?" And Herodias replied, "The head of John the Baptist!"
AMP.25: And she rushed back to the king and asked, saying, "I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptist on a platter!"
AMP.26: The king was deeply grieved, but because of his oaths and his dinner guests [who might have regarded him as weak], he was unwilling to [break his word and] refuse her.
AMP.27: So the king immediately sent for an executioner and commanded him to bring back John's head. And he went and had John beheaded in the prison,
AMP.28: and brought back his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl; and the girl gave it to her mother.
AMP.29: When his disciples heard about this, they came and took away John's body and laid it in a tomb.
AMP.30: The apostles [who had been sent out on a mission] gathered together with Jesus and told Him everything that they had done and taught.
AMP.31: He said to them, "Come away by yourselves to a secluded place and rest a little while"—for there were many [people who were continually] coming and going, and they could not even find time to eat.
AMP.32: And they went away by themselves in the boat to a secluded place.
AMP.33: Many [people] saw them leaving, and recognized them and ran there together on foot from all the [surrounding] cities, and got there ahead of them.
AMP.34: When Jesus went ashore, He saw a large crowd [waiting], and He was moved with compassion for them because they were like sheep without a shepherd [lacking guidance]; and He began to teach them many things.
AMP.35: When the day was nearly gone, His disciples came to Him and said, "This is an isolated place, and it is already late;
AMP.36: send the crowds away so that they may go into the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat."
AMP.37: But He replied, "You give them something to eat!" And they asked Him, "Shall we go and buy 200 denarii worth of bread and give it to them to eat?"
AMP.38: He said to them, "How many loaves do you have? Go look!" And when they found out, they said, "Five [loaves], and two fish."
AMP.39: Then Jesus commanded them all to sit down by groups on the green grass.
AMP.40: They sat down in groups of hundreds and of fifties [so that the crowd resembled an orderly arrangement of colorful garden plots].
AMP.41: Taking the five loaves and two fish, He looked up to heaven and said a blessing [of praise and thanksgiving to the Father]. Then He broke the loaves and [repeatedly] gave them to the disciples to set before the people; and He divided up the two fish among them all.
AMP.42: They all ate and were satisfied.
AMP.43: And the disciples picked up twelve full baskets of the broken pieces [of the loaves], and of the fish.
AMP.44: Those who ate the loaves were five thousand men [not counting the women and children].
AMP.45: Jesus immediately insisted that His disciples get into the boat and go ahead [of Him] to the other side to Bethsaida, while He was dismissing the crowd.
AMP.46: And after He said goodbye to them, He went to the mountain to pray.
AMP.47: Now when evening had come, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and Jesus was alone on the land.
AMP.48: Seeing the disciples straining at the oars, because the wind was against them, at about the fourth watch of the night (3:00-6:00 a.m.) He came to them, walking on the sea. And [acted as if] He intended to pass by them.
AMP.49: But when they saw Him walking on the sea, they thought it was a ghost, and cried out [in horror];
AMP.50: for they all saw Him and were shaken and terrified. But He immediately spoke with them and said, "Take courage! It is I (I Am)! Stop being afraid."
AMP.51: Then He got into the boat with them, and the wind ceased [as if exhausted by its own activity]; and they were completely overwhelmed,
AMP.52: because they had not understood [the miracle of] the loaves [how it revealed the power and deity of Jesus]; but [in fact] their heart was hardened [being oblivious and indifferent to His amazing works].
AMP.53: When they had crossed over [the sea], they reached the land of Gennesaret and anchored at the shore.
AMP.54: They got out of the boat and immediately people recognized Him,
AMP.55: and ran throughout that surrounding countryside and began to carry around on their mats those who were sick, to any place where they heard He was.
AMP.56: And wherever He came into villages, or cities, or the countryside, they were laying the sick in the market places and pleading with Him [to allow them] just to touch the fringe (tassel with a blue cord) of His robe; and all who touched it were healed.

MSG.1: He left there and returned to his hometown. His disciples came along.
MSG.2: On the Sabbath, he gave a lecture in the meeting place. He made a real hit, impressing everyone. "We had no idea he was this good!" they said. "How did he get so wise all of a sudden, get such ability?"
MSG.3: But in the next breath they were cutting him down: "He's just a carpenter—Mary's boy. We've known him since he was a kid. We know his brothers, James, Justus, Jude, and Simon, and his sisters. Who does he think he is?" They tripped over what little they knew about him and fell, sprawling. And they never got any further.

Jesus Sends Out the Twelve
